payload.title: iOS: HubClient is non-Sendable but shared across concurrent withTaskGroup tasks
payload.description:
`AppStore.loadRelationEdges(for:)` (flow-graph relation fetch) captures the
`HubClient` instance into `withTaskGroup` `group.addTask { … }` closures, which
are `@Sendable` and run off the main actor — and fans out N of them
concurrently. `HubClient` is a plain `@Observable final class` (not `Sendable`,
not an actor), so this is a Swift-6 strict-concurrency violation and an actual
concurrent-use-of-one-client data race.

Surfaced during the SCP-103 search review (out of scope there — this is the
flow-graph code path).

Fix options:
- Make `HubClient` `Sendable` (audit its mutable state — `workspaceId`, `token`,
  `caFingerprint`, `isConnected` — and isolate/guard them), or
- Convert it to an `actor`, or
- Capture a sendable snapshot / serialize the requests instead of sharing the
  instance across concurrent tasks.

Repro: build with strict concurrency checking (Swift 6 language mode) — the
`group.addTask` closures capturing `client` raise the diagnostic.
